entries.definition: having an irrational and excessive mistrust or suspicion of others, believing they are trying to harm you without any real evidence
entries.exampleSentence: After the break-in, he became paranoid about home security and installed five cameras.

entries.definition: excessively anxious or worried about something, often unreasonably (informal)
entries.exampleSentence: I'm a bit paranoid about leaving the oven on — I always double-check before going out.

entries.definition: a person who suffers from paranoia or who is excessively suspicious
entries.exampleSentence: He's a real paranoid — he thinks everyone at work is plotting against him.
